summary: The erdos_problems dataset contains 1,217 problems (1,179 open) curated by Terence Tao, but the provided context exposes only limited activity data: two problems (erdos_0001, erdos_0002) each have a single resolved proof issue, and proof-status buckets q1-q10 each hold 100 issues. With only fully-resolved items visible and no open issues per problem, current traction is concentrated on the two documented proofs while the bulk of activity sits in the unlabeled q1-q10 proof-status queues.
problems: erdos_0001: single resolved issue 'Proof of erdos_0001' — closed, no remaining open work
erdos_0002: single resolved issue 'Proof of erdos_0002' — closed, no remaining open work
Proof status queues q1 through q10 each contain 100 issues with no titles, status breakdown, or linkage to specific Erdős problems
No open issues are reported at the per-problem level, suggesting either incomplete tracking or that activity is aggregated only in the q1-q10 buckets
Untitled issues across all entries make it impossible to assess subject matter, difficulty, or solvability
highlights: Two problems (erdos_0001, erdos_0002) have completed proofs, demonstrating the resolution pipeline is functional
Substantial proof activity exists: 1,000 issues distributed evenly across ten proof-status queues (q1-q10)
Dataset is actively maintained by Terence Tao with rich metadata (cash prizes, tags, OEIS references, status) available for deeper prioritization
Even distribution across q1-q10 (100 each) indicates systematic, balanced processing rather than bottlenecks

suggested_todos.title: Add descriptive titles and Erdős-problem IDs to the 1,000 untitled issues in queues q1-q10 to enable triage
Define and document what each proof-status queue (q1-q10) represents (e.g., stage of verification) and surface open vs. resolved counts
Cross-reference q1-q10 issues against cash-prize and OEIS tags to identify high-value, near-complete problems likely solvable soon
Publish the resolved proofs for erdos_0001 and erdos_0002 as reference templates for the broader team
Build a per-problem open-issue tracker so traction can be measured beyond the aggregate q1-q10 buckets
